title: MessagesResource.render_post id collision handling
description:
File: python/src/lichen/coap/resources.py:555-559

The message ID auto-increment logic has issues:
```python
body.setdefault('id', self._next_id)
if isinstance(body['id'], int):
    self._next_id = max(self._next_id + 1, body['id'] + 1)
else:
    self._next_id += 1
```

Problems:
1. If client provides {'id': 999999999}, _next_id jumps to 1 billion unnecessarily
2. String IDs like 'msg-1' bypass the int check but are stored, then used as dict keys
3. _sent[msg_id] at line 564 uses str(body['id']) but _sent_order stores msg_id directly

If multiple clients provide the same numeric ID, earlier messages get overwritten at line 564 since _sent uses ID as key.

Fix: Server should always assign IDs, or validate provided IDs don't conflict.
